Empires & Puzzles Costumed Mielikki Review: The Bear Protector Arrives
Mielikki puts on her bear costume and gets serious. Is the Paladin class and stat boost enough to make her the best 4-star support in the game? Read our full analysis.
Costumed Mielikki Review: Mama Bear Protection Service
Costumed Mielikki: When The Forest Spirit Goes "Mama Bear" Mode

Released in 2023, the "Protector of the Bears" costume transformed Mielikki from a nimble forest sprite into a sturdy frontline guardian. Shifting from Druid to Paladin, this costume took everything good about the original hero—the minions, the mana buff, the attack down—and turned the dial up to 11.

While the base version was a "poor man's Hulda," Costumed Mielikki is simply a "smart player's essential." She remains an Average speed Nature (Green) hero, but with updated stats and a kit optimized for survival.

The costume art is a drastic and adorable shift. The ethereal leaves are gone, replaced by a warm, protective embrace of a bear cub. It visually communicates her new role: she is here to protect the little ones (your team). The Paladin class fits this aesthetic perfectly—she is a tanky guardian of the wild.

The costume bonus combined with the newer stat baseline gives Costumed Mielikki a significant edge. Her base HP of 1571 is outstanding for a 4-star hero, allowing her to reach nearly 2000 HP with Limit Breaks and emblems. This is critical because her minions inherit a percentage of her max HP. The higher her HP, the bigger the shield she gives to everyone else.

The +24% mana gen allows Very Slow heroes (like Franz in Rush wars) to become terrifyingly efficient.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
As with the base version, damage reduction from Fire specials helps her tank against red mono teams.
remains one of the best passives in the game for a healer/support. The automatic heal and mana boost at low health makes her incredibly annoying to kill.
is an upgrade over the base version's Defense Up. It creates a special shield that reduces damage, further cementing her role as a tanky support.
The costume breathed new life into Mielikki, making her relevant even against newer, stronger 4-star heroes. The switch to Paladin was universally praised for making her tougher.

Even in 2026, a 4-star hero that can summon ~400 HP minions for the whole team and boost mana by 24% is relevant. She holds her own against the Construct and Slime families simply because her utility is unique.
She is a staple for 4-star wars, tournaments, and even budget 5-star teams needing a mana booster.
The Costume wins. The stats are higher, the minions are bigger (18% vs 15%), and the mana buff is stronger.

class (Defense boost on damage) is superior to the Druid class for her role. The Druid talent summons a weak Thorn minion that can accidentally overwrite her powerful Sprite minion. The Paladin talent just makes her harder to kill.
The bonus stats (Attack, Defense, HP, Mana) make the costume version statistically superior to almost any Season 1 or 2 hero.
Always use the costume. The only exception is if you absolutely need a Druid for a specific Class Quest, but even then, the costume's raw power might just brute force it.
As a Paladin, Mielikki wants to be as chonky as possible.
Maximizes the Paladin talent's effectiveness and boosts her already high HP for minion scaling.

Unlike her Druid base form, she won't overwrite her own good minions with bad ones.

She doesn't win the game; she stops you from losing it. If your damage dealers die, she can't finish the fight alone.
Absolute God-Tier. The minions are the best "healing" available.

Pair with average speed hitters like
. Her buff pushes them to functional Fast speed.
Heroes that disable minion summoning are her worst nightmare.
While they can't dispel the mana buff (it's on the minion), they can remove her defense buffs or Paladin stacks.
Focus on leveling her immediately. The costume requires leveling the base hero first, but the payoff is immense. She functions as a "fake 5-star" healer/support for your early map progress.

You likely have her Limit Broken. Consider Alpha Aethers (LB2) if you compete for top 100 in Challenge Events or Tournaments. The extra stats directly translate to minion HP, which translates to score preservation.
The community's immediate reaction to the artwork.
"Seems better to me than the original. I don't like that she's a druid, the original, cause her druid minions might push out her mana minions." -
A keen observation from a player realizing the Paladin class superiority.
"I got her base form last year, and just pulled the costume... will be a fast(er) maxing at least!" -
Relief from players who already invested in the base hero.

Costumed Mielikki represents the perfect evolution of a hero. It fixed the minor flaws of the original (the Druid class conflict) and enhanced her strengths (minion size). She is a protective mother bear in every sense of the word, guarding your team with a fierce and fluffy determination.
The definitive version of Mielikki. Wear the bear suit with pride.
